content: We can frame the original task-specific Self-Refine framework as a basic instantiation of a generalized LLM self-reflection mechanism, and hypothesize that injecting multiple inconsistent solving perspectives (from advanced self-reflection research) into Self-Refine's iterative loop can resolve its blind spot of failing to catch flaws that align with the LLM's own inherent biases. This modified framework would retain Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision, while overcoming the common limitation of single-perspective self-feedback that most existing self-refinement approaches suffer from.
